text.title: Django Testing with pytest — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when django-testing is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.reason: Root cause: pytest-django blocks DB access unmarked → failure with 'Database access not allowed'. Fix: Add @pytest.mark.django_db (or module-level pytestmark) to every test that touches the ORM.

antipattern.description: Patching Model.objects.filter / .get with MagicMock to avoid DB access.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Tests the mock, not the code; passes even when querysets are broken; breaks on minor chain changes. Fix: Use @pytest.mark.django_db with savepoint rollback and real factories — fast enough.

antipattern.description: Patching emails.utils.send_mail when consumer is orders.services.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Python binds names at import — orders.services already holds the original reference. Patch has no effect. Fix: Patch the use site: patch('apps.orders.services.send_mail').
Root cause: 5–10x slower (full truncate after each test); adds minutes to CI for no benefit. Fix: Default to plain django_db. Use transaction=True only for on_commit / post-transaction signal tests.
Root cause: A 200 with empty body or missing fields silently passes broken serializers. Fix: Assert status, then assert response.data['<field>'] per contract field.
